title: IVR Cheat Sheet
description: Essential commands and configurations for Interactive Voice Response systems.

commands.description: Initiate an outbound call using Twilio API.
commands.scenario: Use when you need to programmatically make a phone call through Twilio.

commands.command:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<Response>
    <Gather input="dtmf" timeout="10" numDigits="1">
        <Say>Please press 1 for sales, 2 for support.</Say>
    </Gather>
    <Say>We didn't receive any input. Goodbye!</Say>
</Response>
commands.description: XML to gather user input via DTMF tones in an IVR system.
commands.scenario: Use when you need to collect user input in an IVR menu.

commands.command: aws polly synthesize-speech --output-format mp3 --voice-id Joanna --text 'Welcome to our service' welcome.mp3
commands.description: Convert text to speech using AWS Polly.
commands.scenario: Use when you need to generate audio files for IVR prompts.

commands.command:
import nexmo

client = nexmo.Client(key='your_key', secret='your_secret')

response = client.send_ivr_message({'to': '441632960961', 'from': 'Nexmo', 'text': 'Hello, this is a test call'})
commands.description: Send an IVR message using the Nexmo API.
commands.scenario: Use when you need to send automated IVR messages via Nexmo.

commands.description: Play an audio file in a Twilio IVR call.
commands.scenario: Use when you need to play a pre-recorded message in an IVR system.

commands.description: Update a Twilio phone number to handle SMS with a webhook.
commands.scenario: Use when you need to configure a phone number to handle incoming SMS messages.

commands.description: Record a caller's message in a Twilio IVR system.
commands.scenario: Use when you need to record a message from the caller for later review.

commands.description: Make a call using Nexmo API with a specified answer URL.
commands.scenario: Use when you need to initiate a call and direct it to a specific handler.

commands.description: Redirect a call to another URL in a Twilio IVR system.
commands.scenario: Use when you need to transfer a call to another handler or URL.

commands.description: List all completed calls in Twilio.
commands.scenario: Use when you need to review the history of completed calls.

commands.description: Enqueue a call into a TaskRouter workflow in Twilio.
commands.scenario: Use when you need to route calls to specific workflows based on tasks.

commands.description: Create a new call using Twilio CLI.
commands.scenario: Use when you need to initiate a call from the command line.
